content.problemStatement:
Let's define the strength of a matrix as the number of distinct rows in it. Two rows a1,a2,‚Ä¶,ana_1, a_2, \dots, a_na1‚Äč,a2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,an‚Äč and b1,b2,‚Ä¶,bnb_1, b_2, \dots, b_nb1‚Äč,b2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,bn‚Äč are distinct if there is an index iii such that ai‚Č†bia_i \neq b_iai‚ÄčÓÄ†=bi‚Äč.

You are given an integer matrix AAA with NNN rows (numbered 111 through NNN) and MMM columns (numbered 111 through MMM). A submatrix of AAA is formed as the intersection of rows r1r_1r1‚Äč through r2r_2r2‚Äč and columns c1c_1c1‚Äč through c2c_2c2‚Äč for any r1‚Č§r2,c1‚Č§c2r_1 \le r_2, c_1 \le c_2r1‚Äč‚Č§r2‚Äč,c1‚Äč‚Č§c2‚Äč. Find the sum of the strengths of all submatrices of AAA.
constraints: 1‚Č§Ai,j‚Č§109 1 \le A_{i,j} \le 10^91‚Č§Ai,j‚Äč‚Č§109 for each valid i,ji, ji,j
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
NNN lines follow. For each iii (1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N), the iii-th of these lines contains MMM space-separated integers Ai,1,Ai,2,‚Ä¶,Ai,MA_{i, 1}, A_{i, 2}, \dots, A_{i, M}Ai,1‚Äč,Ai,2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,Ai,M‚Äč.
content.outputFormat: Print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the sum of strengths of all the submatrices.
